visual: From a shoulder-width stance, weight transfers slowly side to side between the legs, hips settling into each side without the upper body bobbing.
motion_description: From a stable, shoulder-width stance, the weight is slowly and continuously transferred from one leg to the other. As the weight settles into each side, both arms lower from chest height to rest naturally by the hips. The torso remains upright and forward-facing, moving as a single unit without twisting or rotating. The lateral movement maintains a deliberate, steady tempo, creating an uninterrupted side-to-side flow.
